concept.pre:
Grow up!
1105-383:
The proper adjustment to make on the attitude indicator, Figure 17 during
level flight is to align the:
Miniature airplane to the horizon bar. Horizon bar to the level flight indication. Hoizon bar to the miniature airplane. Banking indicator to the zero-bank indication. 1310-175:
If Coeur D'Alene Tower is not in operation, which frequency should be used to
monitor airport traffic?
119.1 Mhz, CTAF. 122.2/108.8 MHz, Fss. 122.8 MHz, UNICOM 122.9 MHz, MULTICOM 1330-416:
On what bearing should the VOR receiver (OBS) be set in order to navigate
direct from Majors airport to Quitman VORTAC (Figure A-3, Area A to B).
101 108 281 288 1478-210:
What is the recommended communications procedure for landing at Lincoln
Municipal during the hours when the tower is not in operation (See Fig 6)
Contact the Lincoln FSS on 118.5 MHz for traffic advisories. Contact UNICOM on 122.95 MHz for traffic advisories Monitor ATIS for airport conditions, then announce position on 122.95 MHz. Request advisories and sequencing from Minneapolis Center Approach Control. 1707-453:
Upon entering severe turbulence, which condition should the pilot attempt to
maintain?
Level flight attitude Constant altitude Constant airspeed Constant altitude and constant airspeed 1748-292:
What weather is forecast for the Gulf Coast area just ahead of the cold front
during the first 12 hours. (See figure 8)
Marginal VFR to IFR with intermittent thundershowers and rain showers. IFR with moderate or greater turbulence over the coastal areas. Thunderstorm cells moving northeastward ahead of the front. Rain and drizzle dissipating, clearing along the front. 1765-329:
What type clouds normally produce the icing mentioned by the PIREP in the TWEB
(See figure 13).
Stratus Cumulus Stratocumulus Cumulonimbus
